column5: M-25-21, Accelerating Federal Use of AI through Innovation, Governance, and Public Trust
column13: Agency AI governance; high-impact-AI determination; pre-deployment testing; AI impact assessments; monitoring; discontinuation or waiver of noncompliant high-impact uses.

column5: M-24-10, legacy federal AI governance memorandum
column13: Rescinded and replaced by M-25-21.

column5: M-25-22, Driving Efficient Acquisition of Artificial Intelligence in Government
column13: Cross-functional acquisition review; American-made AI policy; privacy; IP and data terms; no unauthorized training; portability and lock-in protection; testing; performance monitoring; disclosure analysis; shared internal repository.
column14: data_ownership; ip_rights; training_prohibition; portability; vendor_lock_in; predeployment_testing; performance_metrics; ai_disclosure; repository_sharing; american_made; privacy
column27: Applies to covered solicitations and options from September 30, 2025. Agency policy updates were due December 29, 2025. Excludes covered national-security systems.

column5: M-24-18, legacy responsible-AI-acquisition memorandum
column13: Rescinded and replaced by M-25-22.

column5: M-26-04, Increasing Public Trust in Artificial Intelligence Through Unbiased AI Principles
column13: Unbiased AI contractual requirements for LLM procurements; vendor transparency documents; governance, evaluation, provenance, and third-party-control disclosures; relevant terms treated as material to eligibility and payment; user reporting process.
column27: The memorandum states its own scheduled sunset of December 11, 2027. This inventory reports the memorandum's requirements as issued and takes no position on them.

column5: Executive Order 14409, Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security
column13: Advanced-AI innovation, cyber defense, system hardening, and protection of US AI intellectual property.
column27: No direct acquisition clause mandate was identified in the fetched text. Published at 91 FR 34565 on June 5, 2026.

column5: National Security Presidential Memorandum 11 (NSPM-11)
column13: Anti-disable, anti-degrade, and anti-unapproved-modification clauses or equivalent controls; reliability, robustness, steerability, controllability; testing and interoperability; contract termination and waivers; multi-vendor procurement updates.

column5: GAO-26-107859 Recommendation 1: systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons learned
column13: Update policy to systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons, including contract-clause practices, and submit them to GSA's repository.

column5: GSA CIO 2185.1C, Accelerating Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence at GSA
column13: Internal GSA assessment, procurement, approval, monitoring, transparency, lifecycle, risk, privacy, security, IP, interoperability, and American-made-AI governance.

column5: State Contracting Manual Chapter 23.03, generative AI contract terms
column13: All acquisition types must include GenAI terms; current model provisions govern new contracts and specified amendments.

column5: State Administrative Manual 4986.9, GenAI disclosure and notification language
column13: GenAI disclosure and notification language for solicitations and contracts; notification when GenAI is a deliverable or materially affects systems, risk, or performance; pre-award risk assessment.
